dialog.stepLabel: A reunion with the Troupe of Fools. Talk to Bardolino
dialog.nodeDesc: 再见愚人剧团播放剧情

items.speaker: Bardolino
items.text: Haha, perfect timing, dear {Male=boy;Female=girl}! I've got a new play in the works, and I'm in dire need of a nimble hero!

items.speaker: Bardolino
items.text: And you, you'd be splendid too! How about playing a young Acolyte, lost and anxious, grappling with her faith and future?

items.speaker: Lenore
items.text: You must be Mr. Bardolino? I recognize you! I used to love watching your performances when I was a child!

items.speaker: Bardolino
items.text: Nonsense, are you saying you don't love watching them now? Well, these days I do walk the wire. Perhaps not everyone's cup of tea.

items.speaker: Lenore
items.text: No, no, I didn't mean it that way... I'm sorry!

items.speaker: Andreas
items.text: Stop teasing her, you old clown.

items.speaker: Bardolino
items.text: Andreas! We've been partners for how long now? Where's the chemistry? My punchline hasn't landed yet!

items.speaker: Andreas
items.text: I'm sorry, Miss. This old man made a bet to tell a hundred jokes in a day. He's still twenty-one jokes short and starting to sweat, ha!

items.speaker: Bardolino
items.text: No, no, the {Male=boy;Female=girl} laughed at what I just said. I myself have become the twenty-first joke, so only twenty more to go now!

items.speaker: Lenore
items.text: Andreas? The Andreas Brizio?

items.speaker: Andreas
items.text: Hang on... Are you one of those debt collectors? Listen, I'm a professional financial advisor! Whatever Captain Brant owes you, it's not valid without a proper IOU!

items.speaker: Lenore
items.text: I'm Lenore. Don't you remember me?

items.speaker: Bardolino
items.text: Or a joke?

items.speaker: Andreas
items.text: Ah, I remember now! You must be Ms. Lenore from the something-something Archive of the Sanctuary Affairs Bureau, right? You sent me sympathy letters after I applied for the Order's aid.

items.speaker: Andreas
items.text: They didn't give me a single penny, but your words back then meant the world. Thank you.

items.speaker: Lenore
items.text: If only I could've done more for you...

items.speaker: Bardolino
items.text: Ha, good thing you didn't! Otherwise, he'd never have ruffled the Order's feathers and ended up on a Pilgrim's Sail branded as a Fool. He'd just be a dull, Credit-counting merchant the rest of his life.

items.speaker: Bardolino
items.text: Well, I admit that one was a bit dark, but a joke is a joke. That's nineteen.

items.speaker: Lenore
items.text: If only I'd noticed the Order's plans sooner. If only I... No, no matter how early, I couldn't have done anything... I'm sorry.

items.speaker: Andreas
items.text: Ah... I see what this is all about. The girl survived, but can't think straight.

items.speaker: Bardolino
items.text: In technical terms, this is known as the Survivor's Guilt-induced Awkwardness Syndrome. Many newcomers to the Troupe of Fools have it. Give her the old treatment, Andreas.

items.speaker: Andreas
items.text: With pleasure!

items.speaker: Andreas
items.text: Now, listen to me carefully, Ms. Lenore—I want you to grab every unhappy thought you've got... and direct it squarely at Captain Brant!

items.speaker: Lenore
items.text: Huh?

items.speaker: Andreas
items.text: Brant said this himself: "We are the Troupe of Fools, tasked with bringing laughter to the world. If even one person is still sad, then that's on me!"

items.speaker: Lenore
items.text: But I don't even know Mr. Brant.

items.speaker: Andreas
items.text: All the more reason it's a Brant Problem. He claims laughter should reach every corner of the world. Well, clearly it missed yours!

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Come to think of it, if Brant had put you on the right ship, you wouldn't have ended up in Mournfell Canyon... So really, it is Brant's fault.

items.speaker: Bardolino
items.text: Oh Brant, how could you do such a thing? For shame!

items.speaker: Lenore
items.text: But I was a member of the Order. If it weren't for the Order...

items.speaker: Andreas
items.text: Ms. Lenore, I don't blame a pure soul for being naive. And I won't fault a kind heart for not being kinder.

items.speaker: Andreas
items.text: If you need a reason to live, let it be this: Your letters once saved me. I, Andreas, hope you'll live on.

items.speaker: Bardolino
items.text: Oh, back in the old days, I'd be playing the Nimbus Chorales at a time like this. Such a shame the song's now lost forever!

items.speaker: Lenore
items.text: No, {PlayerName} and I brought it back!

items.speaker: Lenore
items.text: It's truly a beautiful song!
Before leaving, Andreas hands Lenore a list of the Troupe's upcoming performances. He invites her to swing by anytime for a show... and maybe yell at Brant a little while she's at it.
